nativeRange.habitat: Shallow, protected inshore reefs, estuaries, and sand-weed areas on rocky outcrops; tends to perch on rubble margins and coral formations.

decorPreferences: abundant live rock for perching and grazing
open sand bed with rubble margins

diet.notes: Has been documented as a near-constant algae grazer; does best in mature aquariums with abundant film/turf algae. Generally advised to supplement with dried seaweed (nori), Spirulina, and herbivore preparations to prevent it from nipping at corals when underfed.

avoidWith: lawnmower blenny and other algae-grazing blennies
conspecifics in modest tanks (unless a mated pair)

summary: Mottled brown blenny dusted with white star-like spots; a peaceful, near-constant algae grazer that does best in mature reef tanks with abundant film algae and a tight-fitting lid.
careNotes: Generally considered a hardy, peaceful reef-safe fish that spends most of its time perched on rockwork hopping between grazing spots. Has been known to nip at SPS coral polyps and clam mantles if film/turf algae runs short, so consistent algae availability or daily seaweed supplementation is generally advised. Best kept singly unless the tank is large and a confirmed mated pair is introduced together; tends to harass other similarly-shaped blennies (lawnmower, sailfin, bicolor). Like most Salarias blennies it is a strong jumper, so a tightly-sealed lid or mesh top is essential. Sources surveyed: LiveAquaria, FishBase, and Saltwater Aquarium Blog all describe a 14 cm adult size and broadly similar water parameters; tank-size recommendations spanned 30 US gal (~114 L) at retail to 200+ L in hobbyist guides — the larger figure is used here given the species' near-constant grazing behavior and need for ample algae-covered rockwork.
breedingNotes: Has been reported to spawn in captivity occasionally — oviparous with demersal, adhesive eggs attached via filamentous pedestals; planktonic larvae are difficult to raise outside of dedicated larviculture setups. Not a commonly captive-bred aquarium species.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry source — care level, water parameters, temperament, diet, reef-safe status. Note: 30 US gal minimum quoted by retailer was treated as low-end; larger value chosen per playbook tank-size rule.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (14 cm TL), depth range (1–15 m), taxonomy (Blenniidae, Blenniiformes), native range (Western Central Pacific), and wild temperature range (25.3–29.2 °C).

additional.name: Saltwater Aquarium Blog
additional.notes: Tertiary hobbyist source — confirmed jumping/escape risk (high, lid required), peaceful temperament, herbivore diet, lifespan up to ~6 years in captivity, and a note that grazing behavior may occasionally disturb corals.

fish.breedingNotes: Pelagic larvae are challenging to raise; not commonly bred in home aquaria.
